info.title: Web Application Scanning v2
info.description: The Tenable Web App Scanning v2 API serves as a gateway for you to interact with the Tenable Web App Scanning application and enables users to automate the security management of web applications. You can safely and accurately scan web applications to provide deep visibility into vulnerabilities and context for prioritizing remediations. Use the Tenable Web App Scanning v2 API endpoints to perform CRUD operations on web application scans, launch web application scans, and obtain scan and asset details. For the most up-to-date announcements, subscribe to our [RSS feed](https://feeds.feedburner.com/TenableDeveloperHub) or check the [API Changelog](https://developer.tenable.com/changelog).

tags.name: Applications
tags.description:
With the WAS Applications API, you can create web application assets.

For more information, see the [Targets](https://docs.tenable.com/web-app-scanning/Content/WAS/Scans/BasicSettings.htm#Targets) section on the [Basic Settings](https://docs.tenable.com/web-app-scanning/Content/WAS/Scans/BasicSettings.htm) page in the _Tenable Web App Scanning User Guide_.

tags.name: Attachments
tags.description:
With the Attachments API, you can download auxiliary files generated by plugins during a scan. These attachments provide forensic evidence and deeper context for identified vulnerabilities.

For more information, see the [Tenable Web App Scanning User Guide](https://docs.tenable.com/web-app-scanning/).

tags.name: Configurations
tags.description:
With the Configurations API, you can create and maintain reusable scan settings. These endpoints allow you to perform standard CRUD operations on configuration objects to standardize scanning across your environment.

For more information, see [Scan Templates](https://docs.tenable.com/web-app-scanning/Content/WAS/Scans/WASScanSettings.htm) in the _Tenable Web App Scanning User Guide_.

tags.name: Exports
tags.description: With the Exports API, you can manage asynchronous finding exports. Use these endpoints to initiate export jobs, monitor status, and download results in chunks for integration with external workflow management systems.

tags.name: Filters
tags.description: With the Filters API, you can retrieve available filter metadata for various record types, including scan configurations, templates, and workbench results. Use these endpoints to determine which fields and operators are supported for specific POST-based search queries.

tags.name: Folders
tags.description: With the Folders API, you can organize your scans using default and custom folders. Use these endpoints to create, list, rename, and delete folders to maintain your scan hierarchy.

tags.name: Plugins
tags.description: With the Plugins API, you can access detailed metadata for Tenable Web App Scanning plugins. Retrieve vulnerability counts categorized by application, OWASP category, and severity, as well as scan health metrics.

tags.name: Scans
tags.description:
With the Scans API, you can execute and manage scan operations. Use these endpoints to run scans using existing configurations, monitor progress, retrieve results, or delete scan history.

For more information, see the [Scan Workflow](https://docs.tenable.com/web-app-scanning/Content/WAS/Scans/ScanWorkflow.htm) in the _Tenable Web App Scanning User Guide_.

tags.name: Templates
tags.description:
With the Templates API, you can manage scan and user templates. Use these endpoints to retrieve default settings or create custom templates based on organizational policies. Templates serve as the blueprint for creating new scans.

For more information, see [Scan Template Types](https://docs.tenable.com/web-app-scanning/Content/WAS/Scans/ScannerTemplates.htm) in the _Tenable Web App Scanning User Guide_.

tags.name: Vulnerabilities
tags.description:
With the Vulnerabilities API, you can retrieve and analyze detected vulnerabilities. Use these endpoints to get a filtered list of findings identified during web application scans.

For more information, see [Findings](https://docs.tenable.com/web-app-scanning/Content/WAS/WebApplicationFindings.htm) in the _Tenable Web App Scanning User Guide_.

post.description:
Adds a web application asset. This enables you to manually define application targets with high granularity using a combination of FQDN, port, protocol, and path. Manually defining assets ensures that cumulative scan data is accurately condensed and attributed to the correct application, providing more precise reporting and analysis.
Requires the Basic [16] user role or the `WAS.TOGGLE_WAS.USE` custom role privilege. Additionally, requires the Can View [16] scan permission. See [Roles](doc:roles) and [Permissions](doc:permissions).

200.description: Returned if the web application was added successfully.

400.description: Returned if your request specified invalid parameters or if your request was improperly formatted.

401.description: Returned if the API keys specified in your request are invalid.

403.description: Returned if you do not have permission to add an application. For more information, see [Roles](doc:roles) and [Permissions](doc:permissions).

get.description:
Returns the specified attachment file for a vulnerability detected by a Tenable Web App Scanning scan. Attachments provide additional details for a detected vulnerability. **Note:** The `transfer-encoding` header value of the response stream is set to `chunked`.
Requires the Basic [16] user role or the `WAS.TOGGLE_WAS.USE` custom role privilege. Additionally, requires the Can View [16] scan permission. See [Roles](doc:roles) and [Permissions](doc:permissions).

parameters.name: attachment_id
parameters.description: The UUID of the attachment to download. To determine the UUID of an attachment, use either the [GET /was/v2/vulnerabilities](ref:was-v2-vulns-list) or [GET /was/v2/scans/{scan_id}/vulnerabilities](ref:was-v2-scans-details-vulns) endpoint.

200.description: Returns the specified attachment file as a chunked transfer-encoded stream.

post.description:
Creates a new scan configuration. This operation is asynchronous and returns with an immediate 202. The scan configuration is guaranteed to be created at some point in the immediate future depending on resource load. To track the status of the operation, use the [GET /was/v2/configs/{config_id}/status/{tracking_id}](ref:was-v2-config-status) endpoint. The `config_id` and `tracking_id` can be retrieved from the URI provided in the `Location` header of the `202 Accepted` response.
Requires the Scan Manager [40] user role or the `WAS.TOGGLE_WAS.USE` custom role privilege. Additionally, requires the Can Edit [64] scan permission. See [Roles](doc:roles) and [Permissions](doc:permissions).

get.description:
Returns a scan configuration for the vulnerability that you want to remediate. **Note:** This endpoint does not create the remediation scan configuration. Use the response from this endpoint as the request body for the [Create scan config](ref:was-v2-config-create) endpoint to create the remediation scan.
Requires the Scan Manager [40] user role or the `WAS.TOGGLE_WAS.USE` custom role privilege. Additionally, requires the Can Edit [64] scan permission. See [Roles](doc:roles) and [Permissions](doc:permissions).

parameters.name: vuln_id
parameters.description: The UUID of the vulnerability you want to remediate. You can use the [Search vulnerabilities](ref:was-v2-vulns-search) endpoint or the [Search vulnerabilities for scan](ref:was-v2-scans-details-vulns-search) endpoint to determine the UUID of the vulnerability you want to remediate.

200.description: Returned if Tenable Web App Scanning successfully generated the remediation scan configuration.

403.description: Returned if you do not have permissions to generate a remediation scan configuration. For more information, see [Roles](doc:roles) and [Permissions](doc:permissions).

404.description: Returned if Tenable Web App Scanning could not find the specified vulnerability.

put.description:
Updates an existing scan configuration or creates a new scan configuration. This operation is asynchronous and returns with an immediate 202. The scan configuration is guaranteed to be created at some point in the immediate future depending on resource load. To track the status of the operation, use the [GET /was/v2/configs/{config_id}/status/{tracking_id}](ref:was-v2-config-status) endpoint. The `config_id` and `tracking_id` can be retrieved from the URI provided in the `Location` header of the `202 Accepted` response. **Note:** Although this endpoint can be used to create a scan configuration, Tenable recommends the [POST /was/v2/configs](ref:was-v2-config-create) endpoint instead for the creation of scan configurations. To create a scan configuration with this endpoint, you first need to generate a UUID. Tenable recommends the `uuidgen` tool available in most Linux distributions. The `--time` option can be passed to the `uuidgen` tool to ensure that the UUID is unique.
Requires the Scan Manager [40] user role or the `WAS.SCAN_WAS.MANAGE` custom role privilege. Additionally, requires the Can Edit [64] scan permission. See [Roles](doc:roles) and [Permissions](doc:permissions).

parameters.description: If updating an existing scan configuration, the UUID of the scan configuration you want to update. If creating a new scan configuration, a new UUID generated with a tool like `uuidgen`.

get.description:
Tracks the current status of a scan configuration creation, update, or upsert process. When creating or updating a scan configuration, this endpoint can be used to determine the current status of the request. You can poll this endpoint using a sensible pause duration between each request until a `completed` or `failed` status is returned. The `config_id` and `tracking_id` can be retrieved from the URI provided in the `Location` header of the `202 Accepted` response from the [POST /was/v2/configs](ref:was-v2-config-create) or [PUT /was/v2/configs/{config_id}](ref:was-v2-config-upsert) request used to create or update the scan configuration.
Requires the Scan Operator [24] user role or the `WAS.SCAN_WAS.MANAGE` custom role privilege. Additionally, requires the Can View [16] scan permission. See [Roles](doc:roles) and [Permissions](doc:permissions).

parameters.description:
The UUID of the scan configuration.

**Note:** The `config_id` can be retrieved from the URI provided in the `Location` header of the `202 Accepted` response from the [POST /was/v2/configs](ref:was-v2-config-create) or [PUT /was/v2/configs/{config_id}](ref:was-v2-config-upsert) request used to create or update the scan configuration.
parameters.name: config_id

parameters.description:
The tracking UUID for the request you want to retrieve the status for. 

**Note:** The `tracking_id` can be retrieved from the URI provided in the `Location` header of the `202 Accepted` response from the [POST /was/v2/configs](ref:was-v2-config-create) or [PUT /was/v2/configs/{config_id}](ref:was-v2-config-upsert) request used to create or update the scan configuration.
parameters.name: tracking_id

200.description: Returned if the status for the scan configuration was found.

tracking_status.description: The status of the scan creation, update, or upsert process. Possible values are `processing`, `complete`, or `failed`.

get.description:
Downloads the specified scan configuration in HOCON (Human-Optimized Config Object Notation) format for use with WAS Docker images in CI/CD (continuous integration / continuous delivery) mode. You can integrate Tenable WAS Docker images with GitHub, GitLab, Jenkins, CircleCI, or Bamboo to scan builds and prevent vulnerabilities before your code and applications are deployed. For more information, see [CI/CD Application Scan Overview](https://docs.tenable.com/web-app-scanning/Content/WAS/GettingStarted/Integrations/CI-CD/Overview.htm) in the Tenable Web App Scanning User Guide.
Requires the `WAS.TOGGLE_WAS.USE` custom role privilege. Additionally, requires the Can Configure WAS scan permission. See [Roles](doc:roles) and [Permissions](doc:permissions).

parameters.name: config_id
parameters.description: The UUID of the scan configuration to export in HOCON format for WAS CI/CD mode. You can use the [POST /was/v2/configs/search](ref:was-v2-config-search) endpoint to determine the UUID of the scan configuration you want to export.

200.description: Returned if Tenable Web App Scanning successfully returned the HOCON file for the specified scan configuration.

post.description:
Exports Tenable Web App Scanning findings that match the request criteria. Users need at least BASIC [16] user permissions and the Can View access control permission for the asset objects they want to export. You can set the Can View permission for the All Assets object for all assets. ADMINISTRATOR [64] users can export without the explicit Can View access control permissions. **Note:** There are limits for concurrent export requests. For more information, see [Concurrency Limiting](doc:concurrency-limiting) and [Rate Limiting](doc:rate-limiting).
Requires the Basic [16] user role and the Can View access control permission for the asset objects to be exported. Administrator [64] users can export without the explicit Can View access control permissions. See [Roles](doc:roles).

num_assets.description: Specifies the number of assets used to chunk the findings. The findings export is split up by number of asset IDs in a chunk. The exported data of a chunk is the sum of all the findings for each asset in that chunk. The range for number of assets in a chunk is a minimum of 50 (the default size) to a maximum of 5,000. If you specify a value outside this range, the system uses the upper or lower-bound value.

include_unlicensed.description: Specifies whether or not to include unlicensed assets. The default is `false` when no parameter is specified.

filters.description:
Specifies filters to apply to the findings export.

**Note:** By default, vulnerability exports will only include accepted and non-accepted vulnerabilities found or fixed within the last 30 days if no time-based filters are submitted with the request.

asset_uuid.description: A list of asset UUIDs for which you want to filter findings returned in the findings export.

asset_name.description: An asset name for which you want to filter findings returned in the findings export.

first_found.description: Returns findings that were first found between the specified date and now. The date must be specified in Unix 10-digit time format (seconds).

last_fixed.description: Returns findings that were fixed between the specified date and now. The date must be specified in Unix 10-digit time format (seconds).

last_found.description: Returns findings that were last found between the specified date and now. The date must be specified in Unix 10-digit time format (seconds).

indexed_at.description: Returns findings that were indexed into Tenable Web App Scanning at the specified date. The date must be specified in Unix 10-digit time format (seconds).

ipv4s.description: A list of IPv4 addresses for which for which you want to filter findings returned in the findings export.

owasp_2010.description: A list of chapters from the OWASP Categories 2010 report for which you want to filter findings returned in the findings export.

owasp_2013.description: A list of chapters from the OWASP Categories 2013 report for which you want to filter findings returned in the findings export.

owasp_2017.description: A list of chapters from the OWASP Categories 2017 report for which you want to filter findings returned in the findings export.

owasp_2021.description: A list of chapters from the OWASP Categories 2021 report for which you want to filter findings returned in the findings export.

owasp_api_2019.description: A list of chapters from the OWASP Categories API 2019 report for which you want to filter findings returned in the findings export.

plugin_ids.description: A list of plugin IDs for which you want to filter the findings returned in the findings export. If your request omits this parameter, the export includes all findings regardless of plugin ID.

severity.description:
Returns findings with the specified severity. Defaults to all severity levels. The severity of a finding is defined using the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score. Supported array values are:
 - info—The finding has a CVSS score of 0.
 - low—The finding has a CVSS score between 0.1 and 3.9.
 - medium—The finding has a CVSS score between 4.0 and 6.9.
 - high—The finding has a CVSS score between 7.0 and 9.9.
 - critical—The finding has a CVSS score of 10.0.

severity_modification_type.description:
Returns findings with the specified severity modification type. This filter can be used to return findings with a modified severity due to a recast or accept rule. Supported case-sensitive values are:
 - NONE—No modification to the severity has been made.
 - RECASTED—A user has recast the risk associated with the finding.
 - ACCEPTED—A user has accepted the risk associated with the finding.

since.description:
The start date for the range of data you want to export. The date must be specified in Unix 10-digit time format (seconds). Use this filter in conjunction with the state filter as follows:
 - If the state filter is set to `OPEN` or `REOPENED`, the export includes data for findings that were seen on or after the since date you specify.
 - If the state filter is set to `FIXED`, the export includes data for findings that were fixed on or after the since date you specify.
 - If you do not include the `state` filter in your request, the export includes data for `OPEN` and `REOPENED` findings that were seen on or after the since date you specify, and `FIXED` findings that were fixed on or after the since date you specify.
**Note:** This filter cannot be used in conjunction with the `first_found`, `last_found`, or `last_fixed` filters.

state.description:
Returns findings with the specified state. Supported, case-insensitive values are:
 - OPEN—The finding is currently present on an asset.
 - REOPENED—The finding was previously marked as fixed on an asset but has returned.
 - FIXED—The finding was present on an asset but is no longer detected.

This parameter is required if your request includes `first_found`, `last_found`, or `last_fixed` parameters. If your request omits this parameter, the export includes default states `OPEN` and `REOPENED` only.

Note that the API uses different terms for finding states than the user interface. The `new` and `active` states in the user interface map to the `OPEN` state in the API. The `resurfaced` state in the user interface maps to the `REOPENED` state in the API. The `FIXED` state is the same.

get.description:
Downloads exported WAS findings as a JSON file. The response content type is `application/octet-stream`. Chunks are available for download for up to 24 hours after they have been created. Tenable Web App Scanning returns a 404 message for expired chunks. Export chunks do not include an attribute if that attribute is empty in the vulnerability record. A successful response message contains attributes that correspond to CVSS metrics; these metrics are described fully in the following documents:
  • [A Complete Guide to the Common Vulnerability Scoring System, Version 2.0](https://www.first.org/cvss/v2/guide)
  • [Common Vulnerability Scoring System v3.0: Specification Document](https://www.first.org/cvss/specification-document)

value.name: Backup file
value.solution: Do not keep obsolete versions of files under the virtual web server root.
value.description:
A common practice when administering web applications is to create a copy/backup
  of a particular file or directory prior to making any modification to the file.
  Another common practice is to add an extension or change the name of the original
  file to signify that it is a backup (examples include `.bak`, `.orig`, `.backup`,
  etc.).

  During the initial recon stages of an attack, cyber-criminals will attempt to
  locate backup files by adding common extensions onto files already discovered on
  the webserver. By analysing the response headers from the server they are able to
  determine if the backup file exists.
  These backup files can then assist in the compromise of the web application.

  By utilising the same method, scanner was able to discover a possible backup file.

findings.description:
Referrer Policy provides mechanisms to websites to restrict referrer information (sent in the referer header) that browsers will be allowed to add.

No Referrer Policy header or metatag configuration has been detected.
findings.solution: Configure Referrer Policy on your website by adding 'Referrer-Policy' HTTP header or meta tag referrer in HTML.

findings.name: Missing Subresource Integrity (SRI)
findings.description:
Subresource Integrity (SRI) is a web security standard that enables browsers to verify that resources hosted by third parties (CDN for example) are delivered without unexpected manipulation.

SRI works by comparing a cryptographic hash declared in the integrity attribute of resource tag (like script) used to fetch the resource and the calculated hash value of this resource.

No SRI have been detected for one or more resources.
findings.solution: Add integrity attribute to the resource tag with prefixed and base64 encoded hash of the resource.

findings.name: Missing 'X-Frame-Options' Header
findings.description:
Clickjacking (User Interface redress attack, UI redress attack, UI redressing) is a malicious technique of tricking a Web user into clicking on something different from what the user perceives they are clicking on, thus potentially revealing confidential information or taking control of their computer while clicking on seemingly innocuous web pages.

The server didn't return an `X-Frame-Options` header which means that this website could be at risk of a clickjacking attack.

The `X-Frame-Options` HTTP response header can be used to indicate whether or not a browser should be allowed to render a page inside a frame or iframe. Sites can use this to avoid clickjacking attacks, by ensuring that their content is not embedded into other sites.
findings.solution: Configure your web server to include an `X-Frame-Options` header.
findings.output: Page http://192.0.2.214/ has no X-Frame-Option header defined

findings.name: Missing 'X-Content-Type-Options' Header
findings.description:
The HTTP 'X-Content-Type-Options' response header prevents the browser from MIME-sniffing a response away from the declared content-type.

The server did not return a correct 'X-Content-Type-Options' header, which means that this website could be at risk of a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attack.
findings.solution: Configure your web server to include an 'X-Content-Type-Options' header with a value of 'nosniff'.

ui_forms.description: Indicates whether to scan input and button groups associated with JavaScript code (`true`).

ui_inputs.description: Indicates whether to scan orphan input elements with associated DOM events (`true`).

headers.description: Indicates whether to scan headers for vulnerabilities and insecure configurations (`true`); for example, missing X-Frame-Options.

links.description: Indicates whether to scan links and their parameters in vulnerability checks (`true`).

parameter_names.description: Indicates whether to perform extensive fuzzing of parameter names (`true`).

parameter_values.description: Indicates whether to perform performs extensive fuzzing of parameter values (`true`).

jsons.description: Indicates whether to scan JSON request data (`true`).

xmls.description: Indicates whether to scan XML request data (`true`).

cookies_extensively.description: Indicates whether an assessment is enabled (`true`) or disabled (`false`).

with_raw_payloads.description: Indicates whether parameters should be tested with their raw payloads (`true`) or not (`false`).

with_both_http_methods.description: Indicates if both GET and POST methods should be used during an assessment (`true`) or not (`false`).

with_extra_parameter.description: Indicates whether to test non-existing parameters (`true`) or disabled (`false`).

ScanStatus.description:
The scan status. Values include:
 - aborted—Terminated before completion for reason other than a user-requested stop.
 - canceled—Stopped by a user request.
 - completed—Completed successfully.
 - pending—Start requested but not yet running.
 - processing—The scan has completed but the results are still being processed. The scanner is processing vulnerability findings, attachments, notes, and other metadata.
 - ready—Scan is ready to be started.
 - running—Scan is in progress.
 - stopping—Stop requested but not yet stopped.

plugin_state.description:
Indicates whether the user of the Tenable-provided template has the ability to redefine the settings for the template. Possible values are: 
 - `locked` -- You cannot modify the template. Tenable Web App Scanning rejects any request submitted with settings that differ from the defined template settings.
 - `open` -- You can modify the template without restriction

scanner_types.description:
Indicates which scanners you can use to run scans based on the Tenable-provided template. Possible values are: 
 - `scanner` -- A single scanner running on premises in the client's own infrastructure or environment. 
 - `container_group` -- A group of client scanners running on premises. 
 - `cloud-group` -- A group of managed scanners running within Tenable's cloud infrastructure.

input_type.description: The type of HTML Form input associated with the finding.

proof.description: The output from the web application corroborating that the finding is present.

payload.description: The attack payload used to detect the finding.

state.description:
The state of the finding as determined by the Tenable Web App Scanning state service. Possible values include:
 - OPEN—The finding is currently present on an asset.
 - REOPENED—The finding was previously marked as fixed on an asset but has returned.
 - FIXED—The finding was present on an asset but is no longer detected.

Note that the API uses different terms for finding states than the user interface. The `new` and `active` states in the user interface map to the `OPEN` state in the API. The `resurfaced` state in the user interface maps to the `REOPENED` state in the API. The `FIXED` state is the same.

severity.description: The severity of the finding as defined using the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score. Possible values include `info` (CVSS score of 0), `low` (CVSS score between 0.1 and 3.9), `medium` (CVSS score between 4.0 and 6.9), `high` (CVSS score between 7.0 and 9.9), and `critical` (CVSS score of 10.0).

severity_id.description:
The code for the severity assigned when a user recast the risk associated with the finding. Possible values include: 
 - 0—The vulnerability has a CVSS score of 0, which corresponds to the "info" severity level.
 - 1—The vulnerability has a CVSS score between 0.1 and 3.9, which corresponds to the "low" severity level.
 - 2—The vulnerability has a CVSS score between 4.0 and 6.9, which corresponds to the "medium" severity level.
 - 3—The vulnerability has a CVSS score between 7.0 and 9.9, which corresponds to the "high" severity level.
 - 4—The vulnerability has a CVSS score of 10.0, which corresponds to the "critical" severity level.

severity_default_id.description: The code for the severity originally assigned to a finding before a user recast the risk associated with the finding. Possible values are the same as for the `severity_id` attribute.

recast_reason.description: The text that appears in the Comment field of the recast rule in the Tenable Web App Scanning user interface.

recast_rule_uuid.description: The UUID of the recast rule that applies to the plugin.

severity_modification_type.description:
The type of modification a user made to the finding's severity. Possible values include: 
 - none—No modification has been made.
 - recasted—A user in the Web App Scanning user interface has recast the risk associated with the finding. 
 - accepted—A user in the Web App Scanning user interface has accepted the risk associated with the finding. 

For more information about recast and accept rules, see the [Recast/Accept Rules](https://docs.tenable.com/vulnerability-management/Content/Settings/Recast/AboutRecastRules.htm) in the Tenable Vulnerability Management User Guide.

first_found.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when a scan first detected the finding on the asset.

last_found.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when a scan last detected the finding on the asset.

last_fixed.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when a scan no longer detects the previously detected finding on the asset.

last_observed.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the finding was previously detected/observed on the asset.

indexed_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the vulnerability was indexed into Tenable Web App Scanning.

was-scan-information.description: Information about the latest scan that detected the vulnerability.

schedule_uuid.description: The schedule UUID for the scan that found the vulnerability.

started_at.description: The ISO timestamp when the scan started.

completed_at.description: The ISO timestamp when the scan completed.

uuid.description: The UUID of the scan that found the vulnerability.

was_plugin.description: Information about the plugin that detected the finding.

id.description: The ID of the plugin that identified the finding.

risk_factor.description:
The risk factor of the finding associated with the plugin. The risk factor is determined based on the calculation of the CVSS score, possible values are:
  • `Low`—The vulnerability has a CVSS score between 0.1 and 3.9
  • `Medium`—The vulnerability has a CVSS score between 4.0 and 6.9
  • `High`—The vulnerability has a CVSS score between 7.0 and 9.9
  • `Critical`—The vulnerability has a CVSS score of 10.0
.

original_risk_factor_num.description: The code for the severity originally assigned to a plugin.

type.description: The general type of plugin check (for example, `local` or `remote`).

name.description: The name of the plugin that identified the vulnerability.

version.description: The version of the plugin used to perform the check.

publication_date.description: A timestamp in ISO 8601 format indicating the date and time the plugin was first published.

modification_date.description: A timestamp in ISO 8601 format indicating the date and time the plugin was last updated.

synopsis.description: Brief description of the plugin or vulnerability.

description.description: Full text description of the vulnerability.

patch_publication_date.description: A timestamp in ISO 8601 format indicating the date and time the vendor published a patch for the vulnerability.

exploitability_ease.description: Description of how easy it is to exploit the issue.

stig_severity.description: Security Technical Implementation Guide (STIG) severity code for the vulnerability.

in_the_news.description: A value specifying whether this plugin has received media attention (for example, ShellShock, Meltdown).

exploited_by_malware.description: The finding discovered by this plugin is known to be exploited by malware.

epss_score.description:
The Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) score estimates the likelihood (as a percentage) that a software vulnerability will be exploited in the wild within the next 30 days. The score ranges from 0 to 100, with higher values indicating a greater probability of exploitation. For more information, see FIRST's [Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS)](https://www.first.org/epss/).

**Caution:** The `epss_score` property is currently only available for Vulnerability Management exports. It will be added to Web App Scanning findings exports soon.

cvss2_base_score.description: The CVSSv2 base score (intrinsic and fundamental characteristics of a finding that are constant over time and user environments).

cvss2_temporal_score.description: The CVSSv2 temporal score (characteristics of a finding that change over time but not among user environments).

cvss3_base_score.description: The CVSSv3 base score (intrinsic and fundamental characteristics of a finding that are constant over time and user environments).

cvss3_temporal_score.description: The CVSSv3 temporal score (characteristics of a finding that change over time but not among user environments).

cvss4_base_score.description: The CVSS version 4 (CVSSv4) base score, representing the intrinsic and fundamental characteristics of a vulnerability that remain constant over time and across different user environments. The score ranges from 0 to 10, with higher values indicating greater severity. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

see_also.description: Links to external websites that contain helpful information about the vulnerability.

bid.description: The Bugtraq ID for the plugin.

xrefs.description: References to third-party information about the finding, exploit, or update associated with the plugin. Each reference includes a type and an ID. For example, 'FEDORA' and '2003-047'.

cpe.description: The Common Platform Enumeration (CPE) number for the plugin.

cve.description: The Common Vulnerability and Exposure (CVE) ID for the plugin.

cwe.description: The Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) ID for the plugin.

wasc.description: The Web Application Security Consortium (WASC) ID for the plugin.

owasp_2010.description: A list of chapters in OWASP Categories 2010 report for the plugin.

owasp_2013.description: A list of chapters in OWASP Categories 2013 report for the plugin.

owasp_2017.description: A list of chapters in OWASP Categories 2017 report for the plugin.

owasp_2021.description: A list of chapters in OWASP Categories 2021 report for the plugin.

owasp_api_2019.description: A list of chapters in OWASP Categories API 2019 report for the plugin.

exploit_frameworks.description: A list of exploit frameworks which can exploit findings reported by plugin.

vpr.description: Information about the Vulnerability Priority Rating (VPR) for the vulnerability.

score.description: The Vulnerability Priority Rating (VPR) for the vulnerability. If a plugin is designed to detect multiple vulnerabilities, the VPR represents the highest value calculated for a vulnerability associated with the plugin. For more information, see Severity vs. VPR in the Tenable Vulnerability Management User Guide.

drivers.description: The key drivers Tenable uses to calculate a vulnerability's VPR. For more information, see [Vulnerability Priority Rating Drivers](doc:vpr-drivers-tio).

age_of_vuln.description:
A range representing the number of days since the National Vulnerability Database (NVD) published the vulnerability. Ranges include:
  • 0-7 days
  • 7-30 days
  • 30-60 days
  • 60-180 days
  • 180-365 days
  • 365-730 days
  • More than 730 days (+731)

lower_bound.description: The lower bound of the range. For example, for the 0-7 days range, this attribute is `0`. For the highest range (more than 730 days), this value is `731`.

upper_bound.description: The upper bound of the range. For example, for the 0-7 days range, this attribute is `7`. For the highest range (more than 730 days), this value is `0`, which signifies that there is no higher category.

exploit_code_maturity.description: The relative maturity of a possible exploit for the vulnerability based on the existence, sophistication, and prevalence of exploit intelligence from internal and external sources (for example, Reversinglabs, Exploit-db, Metasploit, etc.). The possible values (`High`, `Functional`, `PoC`, or `Unproven`) parallel the CVSS Exploit Code Maturity categories.

cvss_impact_score_predicted.description: A value specifying whether Tenable predicted the CVSSv3 impact score for the vulnerability because NVD did not provide one (`true`) or used the NVD-provided CVSSv3 impact score (`false`) when calculating the VPR.

cvss3_impact_score.description: The NVD-provided CVSSv3 impact score for the vulnerability. If the NVD did not provide a score, Vulnerability Management displays a Tenable-predicted score.

threat_intensity_last28.description: The relative intensity based on the number and frequency of recently observed threat events related to this vulnerability: `Very Low`, `Low`, `Medium`, `High`, or `Very High`.

threat_sources_last28.description: A list of all sources (for example, social media channels, the dark web, etc.) where threat events related to this vulnerability occurred.

product_coverage.description: The relative number of unique products affected by the vulnerability: `Low`, `Medium`, `High`, or `Very High`.

threat_recency.description:
A range representing the number of days since a threat event occurred for the vulnerability. Ranges include:
  • 0-7 days
  • 7-30 days
  • 30-120 days
  • 120-365 days
  • More than 365 days (+365)

lower_bound.description: The lower bound of the range. For example, for the 0-7 days range, this attribute is `0`. For the highest range (more than 365 days), this value is `366`.

upper_bound.description: The upper bound of the range. For example, for the 0-7 days range, this attribute is `7`. For the highest range (more than 730 days), this value is `0`, which signifies that there is no higher category.

updated.description: The ISO timestamp when v last imported the VPR for this vulnerability. v imports a VPR value the first time you scan a vulnerability on your network. Then, Tenable Web App Scanning automatically re-imports new and updated VPR values daily.

vpr_v2.description: Details about the Vulnerability Priority Rating (VPR) version 2 for the vulnerability. This enhanced version is labeled **VPR (Beta)** in the user interface. For details, see [Vulnerability Priority Rating](https://docs.tenable.com/vulnerability-management/Content/Explore/Findings/RiskMetrics.htm#Vulnerability-Priority-Rating-) in the _Tenable Vulnerability Management User Guide_. To learn about the enhancements introduced in VPR v2, refer to the whitepaper [Enhancements to Tenable Vulnerability Priority Rating (VPR)](https://www.tenable.com/whitepapers/enhancements-to-tenable-vulnerability-priority-rating-vpr).

score.description:
The Vulnerability Priority Rating (VPR) version 2 score assigned to the vulnerability. This enhanced version is labeled **VPR (Beta)** in the user interface. If a plugin detects multiple vulnerabilities, this score reflects the highest VPR among them.

For details about VPR, see [Vulnerability Priority Rating](https://docs.tenable.com/vulnerability-management/Content/Explore/Findings/RiskMetrics.htm#Vulnerability-Priority-Rating-) in the _Tenable Vulnerability Management User Guide_. For details on improvements introduced in VPR v2, see the whitepaper [Enhancements to Tenable Vulnerability Priority Rating (VPR)](https://www.tenable.com/whitepapers/enhancements-to-tenable-vulnerability-priority-rating-vpr).

canvas_package.description: The name of the CANVAS exploit pack that includes the vulnerability.

checks_for_default_account.description: A value specifying whether the plugin checks for default accounts.

checks_for_malware.description: A value specifying whether the plugin checks for malware.

d2_elliot_name.description: The name of the exploit in the D2 Elliot Web Exploitation framework.

exploit_available.description: A value specifying whether a public exploit exists for the vulnerability.

exploit_framework_canvas.description: A value specifying whether an exploit exists in the Immunity CANVAS framework.

exploit_framework_core.description: A value specifying whether  an exploit exists in the CORE Impact framework.

exploit_framework_d2_elliot.description: A value specifying whether  an exploit exists in the D2 Elliot Web Exploitation framework.

exploit_framework_exploithub.description: A value specifying whether  an exploit exists in the ExploitHub framework.

exploit_framework_metasploit.description: A value specifying whether  an exploit exists in the Metasploit framework.

exploited_by_nessus.description: A value specifying whether Nessus exploited the vulnerability during the process of identification.

exploithub_sku.description: The SKU number of the exploit in the ExploitHub framework.

family.description: The family to which plugin belongs.

family_id.description: The ID of the plugin family.

has_patch.description: A value specifying whether the vendor has published a patch for the vulnerability.

metasploit_name.description: The name of the related exploit in the Metasploit framework.

ms_bulletin.description: The Microsoft security bulletin that the plugin covers.

unsupported_by_vendor.description: Software found by this plugin is unsupported by the software's vendor (for example, Windows 95 or Firefox 3).

usn.description: Ubuntu security notice that the plugin covers.

vuln_publication_date.description: A timestamp in ISO 8601 format indicating the date and time the vulnerability was first publicly disclosed. This represents the initial public availability of information about the vulnerability

workaround.description: A description that explains a workaround for remediating the vulnerability.

workaround_type.description:
The workaround action required to remediate the vulnerability. Possible workaround types include: 
 - `Configuration Change` — You must alter the configuration of software on the asset.
 - `Disable Service` — You must disable a service on the asset.

workaround_published.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time when the workaround was published.

vendor_unpatched.description: Indicates if the vulnerability has an available patch from the vendor.

has_workaround.description: Indicates if a workaround exists for the vulnerability.

response-was-export-chunk-asset.description: Information about the asset where the scan detected the vulnerability.

uuid.description: The UUID of the asset where a scan found the vulnerability.

fqdn.description: The fully-qualified domain name of the asset where a scan found the vulnerability.

ipv4.description: The IPv4 address of the asset where a scan found the vulnerability.

ipv4s.description: The list of IPv4 addresses if the asset has multiple IPv4 addresses.

response-findings-export-status.description: Information about the specified findings export job.

uuid.description: The UUID of the findings export request.

status.description:
The status of the findings export request. Possible values include:
 - QUEUED—Tenable Web App Scanning has queued the export request until other requests currently in process are completed.
 - PROCESSING—Tenable Web App Scanning has started processing the export request.
 - FINISHED—Tenable Web App Scanning has completed processing the export request. The list of chunks is complete.
 - CANCELLED—An administrator has cancelled the export request.
 - ERROR—Tenable Web App Scanning encountered an error while processing the export request. Tenable recommends that you retry the request. If the status persists on retry, contact Tenable Support.

chunks_available.description: A list of completed chunks available for download.

chunks_failed.description: A list of chunks for which the export process failed. If a chunk fails processing, submit the export request again. If the chunk continues to fail, contact Tenable Support.

chunks_cancelled.description: A list of chunks for which the export process was cancelled. If a chunk fails processing, Tenable Web App Scanning automatically cancels all subsequent chunks queued for export in the same request.

total_chunks.description: The total number of chunks associated with the export job as a whole.

chunks_available_count.description: The total number of completed chunks available for download.

empty_chunks_count.description: The total number of empty chunks.

finished_chunks.description: The number of chunks that have been processed and are available for download.

filters.description: The filters used in the findings export job request. For a list of possible filters, see the filters available for the [Export findings](ref:was-export-findings) endpoint.

num_assets_per_chunk.description: The number of assets contained in each export chunk.

created.description: A Unix timestamp indicating the date and time on which the exports job was created.

vpr_filter.description:
Filters vulnerabilities based on their Vulnerability Priority Rating (VPR) version 1 score. You can define score ranges using properties such as `gte` (greater than or equal to) and `lte` (less than or equal to). For example, to match vulnerabilities with VPR scores between 9.0 and 9.9, include `{ "gte": 9.0, "lte": 9.9 }` in the filter.



For details about VPR, see [Vulnerability Priority Rating](https://docs.tenable.com/vulnerability-management/Content/Explore/Findings/RiskMetrics.htm#Vulnerability-Priority-Rating-) in the _Tenable Vulnerability Management User Guide_.

eq.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR exactly equal to the specified score or scores. This property _cannot_ be used in combination with `lt`, `gt`, `lte`, or `gte`.

neq.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR _not_ equal to the specified score or scores. This property can be combined with the `eq` property.

gt.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR _greater than_ the specified score. This property cannot be combined with the `eq` property.

gte.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR _greater than or equal to_ the specified score. This property cannot be combined with the `eq` property.

lt.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR _less than_ the specified score. This property cannot be combined with the `eq` property.

lte.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR _less than or equal to_ the specified score. This property cannot be combined with the `eq` property.

vpr_v2_filter.description:
Filters vulnerabilities based on their Vulnerability Priority Rating (VPR) version 2 score. This enhanced version is labeled **VPR (Beta)** in the user interface. You can define score ranges using properties such as `gte` (greater than or equal to) and `lte` (less than or equal to). For example, to match vulnerabilities with VPR scores between 9.0 and 9.9, include `{ "gte": 9.0, "lte": 9.9 }` in the filter.



For details about VPR, see [Vulnerability Priority Rating](https://docs.tenable.com/vulnerability-management/Content/Explore/Findings/RiskMetrics.htm#Vulnerability-Priority-Rating-) in the _Tenable Vulnerability Management User Guide_. To learn about the enhancements introduced in VPR v2, refer to the whitepaper [Enhancements to Tenable Vulnerability Priority Rating (VPR)](https://www.tenable.com/whitepapers/enhancements-to-tenable-vulnerability-priority-rating-vpr).

eq.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR exactly equal to the specified score or scores. This property _cannot_ be used in combination with `lt`, `gt`, `lte`, or `gte`.

neq.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR _not_ equal to the specified score or scores. This property can be combined with the `eq` property.

gt.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR _greater than_ the specified score. This property cannot be combined with the `eq` property.

gte.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR _greater than or equal to_ the specified score. This property cannot be combined with the `eq` property.

lt.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR _less than_ the specified score. This property cannot be combined with the `eq` property.

lte.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a VPR _less than or equal to_ the specified score. This property cannot be combined with the `eq` property.

epss_filter.description:
Filters vulnerabilities by their Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) score, expressed as a percentage from 0 to 100. You can combine properties to define value ranges. For example, use `gte: 50` and `lte: 80` to return vulnerabilities with EPSS scores between 50 and 80 inclusive. 

For more information, see FIRST's [Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS)](https://www.first.org/epss/).

**Note:** This filter is not yet available but will be supported soon.

eq.description: Returns vulnerabilities with an EPSS score equal to the specified value(s). Cannot be combined with `lt`, `gt`, `lte`, or `gte`.

neq.description: Returns vulnerabilities with an EPSS score _not_ equal to the specified value(s). Can be combined with `eq`.

gt.description: Returns vulnerabilities with an EPSS score _greater than_ the specified value. Cannot be combined with `eq`.

gte.description: Returns vulnerabilities with an EPSS score _greater than or equal to_ the specified value. Cannot be combined with `eq`.

lt.description: Returns vulnerabilities with an EPSS score _less than_ the specified value. Cannot be combined with `eq`.

lte.description: Returns vulnerabilities with an EPSS score _less than or equal to_ the specified value. Cannot be combined with `eq`.

cvss4_filter.description:
Filters vulnerabilities based on their CVSS version 4 (CVSSv4) base score. You can specify score ranges using the provided comparison properties. For example, to retrieve vulnerabilities with a CVSSv4 score between 7.0 and 9.9, include `{ "gte": 7.0, "lte": 9.9 }` in your filter.

For more details about CVSSv4, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

eq.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a CVSSv4 score exactly equal to the specified value(s). Cannot be combined with `lt`, `gt`, `lte`, or `gte`.

neq.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a CVSSv4 score _not_ equal to the specified value(s). Can be combined with eq.

gt.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a CVSSv4 score _greater than_ the specified value. Cannot be combined with `eq`.

gte.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a CVSSv4 score _greater than or equal to_ the specified value. Cannot be combined with `eq`.

lt.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a CVSSv4 score _less than_ the specified value. Cannot be combined with `eq`.

lte.description: Returns vulnerabilities with a CVSSv4 score _less than or equal to_ the specified value. Cannot be combined with `eq`.

uuid.description: The UUID of the findings export request.

status.description:
The status of the findings export request. Possible values include:
 - QUEUED—Tenable Web App Scanning has queued the export request until other requests currently in process are completed.
 - PROCESSING—Tenable Web App Scanning has started processing the export request.
 - FINISHED—Tenable Web App Scanning has completed processing the export request. The list of chunks is complete.
 - CANCELLED—An administrator has cancelled the export request.
 - ERROR—Tenable Web App Scanning encountered an error while processing the export request. Tenable recommends that you retry the request. If the status persists on retry, contact Tenable Support.

total_chunks.description: The total number of chunks associated with the export job as a whole.

chunks_available_count.description: The total number of completed chunks available for download.

empty_chunks_count.description: The total number of empty chunks.

finished_chunks.description: The number of chunks that have been processed and are available for download.

filters.description: The filters used in the findings export job request. For a list of possible filters, see the filters available for the [Export findings](ref:was-export-findings) endpoint.

num_assets_per_chunk.description: The number of assets contained in each export chunk.

created.description: A Unix timestamp indicating the date and time on which the exports job was created.

type.description: Framework type/name. Eg. canvas, metasploit, exploithub, etc.

cvss3_temporal_vector.description: CVSSv3 temporal metrics for the vulnerability.

exploitability.description:
The CVSSv3 Exploit Maturity Code (E) for the vulnerability the plugin covers. Possible values include: 
 - Unproven—Corresponds to the Unproven (U) value for the E metric
 - Proof-of-concept—Corresponds to the Proof-of-Concept (POC) value for the E metric
 - Functional—Corresponds to the Functional (F) value for the E metric
 - High—Corresponds to the High (H) value for the E metric
 - Not-defined—Corresponds to the Not Defined (ND) value for the E metric

remediation_level.description:
The CVSSv3 Remediation Level (RL) temporal metric for the vulnerability the plugin covers. Possible values include: 
 - O—Official Fix
 - T—Temporary Fix
 - W—Workaround
 - U—Unavailable
 - X—Not Defined

report_confidence.description:
The CVSSv3 Report Confidence (RC) temporal metric for the vulnerability the plugin covers. Possible values include: 
 - U—Unknown
 - R—Reasonable
 - C—Confirmed
 - X—Not Defined

raw.description: The complete `cvss3_temporal_vector` metrics and result values for the vulnerability the plugin covers in a condensed and coded format. For example, `E:U/RL:OF/RC:C`.

cvss3_vector.description: Additional CVSSv3 metrics for the vulnerability.

access_vector.description:
The CVSSv2 Attack Vector (AV) metric for the vulnerability the plugin covers. Possible values include:
 - Network—Corresponds to the Network (N) value for the AV metric. 
 - Adjacent Network—Corresponds to the Adjacent Network (A) value for the AV metric. 
 - Local—Corresponds to the Local (L) value for the AV metric

access_complexity.description:
The CVSSv3 Access Complexity (AC) metric for the vulnerability the plugin covers. Possible values include: 
 - H—High
 - M—Medium
 - L—Low

authentication.description:
The CVSSv2 Authentication (Au) metric for the vulnerability the plugin covers. Possible values include:
 - None required—Corresponds to the None (N) value for the Au metric. 
 - Requires-single-instance—Corresponds to the Single (S) value for the Au metric. 
 - Requires-multiple-instances—Corresponds to the Multiple (M) value for the Au metric

confidentiality_impact.description:
The CVSSv3 confidentiality impact metric of the vulnerability the plugin covers to the vulnerable component. Possible values include: 
 - H—High
 - L—Low
 - N—None

integrity_impact.description:
The CVSSv3 integrity impact metric for the vulnerability the plugin covers. Possible values include:
 - H—High
 - L—Low
 - N—None

availability_impact.description:
The CVSSv2 availability impact metric for the vulnerability the plugin covers. Possible values include: 
 - H—High
 - L—Low
 - N—None

raw.description: The complete `cvss3_vector` metrics and result values for the vulnerability the plugin covers in a condensed and coded format. For example, `AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C`.

cvss4_vector.description: An object representing the full set of CVSS v4.0 base metrics for the vulnerability. These metrics define the intrinsic characteristics of the vulnerability, including how it can be exploited (e.g., attack vector, privileges required) and the potential impacts to both the vulnerable system and subsequent systems (e.g., confidentiality, integrity, availability). For the complete specification, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

attack_vector.description: The CVSSv4 Attack Vector (AV) metric, which describes the context by which the vulnerability can be exploited. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

attack_complexity.description: The CVSSv4 Attack Complexity (AC) metric, which indicates the conditions beyond the attacker's control that must exist to exploit the vulnerability. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

attack_requirements.description: The CVSSv4 Attack Requirements (AR) metric specifies prerequisite conditions of the vulnerable component that must be met for an attack to succeed. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

privileges_required.description: The CVSSv4 Privileges Required (PR) metric indicates the level of privileges an attacker must have to successfully exploit the vulnerability. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

user_interaction.description: The CVSSv4 User Interaction (UI) metric describes the level of user interaction required for an attack to succeed. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

vulnerable_system_confidentiality.description: The CVSSv4 Vulnerable System Confidentiality (VC) metric measures the impact on the confidentiality of information on the vulnerable system. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

vulnerable_system_integrity.description: The CVSSv4 Vulnerable System Integrity (VI) metric measures the impact on the integrity of information on the vulnerable system. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

vulnerable_system_availability.description: The CVSSv4 Vulnerable System Availability (VA) metric measures the impact on the availability of the vulnerable system. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

subsequent_system_confidentiality.description: The CVSSv4 Subsequent System Confidentiality (SC) metric measures the impact on the confidentiality of information on systems _subsequent_ to the vulnerable component. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

subsequent_system_integrity.description: The CVSSv4 Subsequent System Integrity (SI) metric measures the impact on the integrity of information on systems *subsequent* to the vulnerable component. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

subsequent_system_availability.description: The CVSSv4 Subsequent System Availability (SA) metric measures the impact on the availability of systems _subsequent_ to the vulnerable component. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

raw.description: The complete CVSSv4 Base vector string representing all metric values in a compact, standardized format. This includes metrics such as Attack Vector, Attack Complexity, Privileges Required, and others. For example, `AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:H/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:H`. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

cvss4_threat_vector.description:
An object representing the CVSS v4.0 Threat metrics for the vulnerability. These metrics provide context on current, observed threat activity in the wild, such as evidence of exploitation or the presence of available exploit code. Threat metrics can help refine the severity and prioritization of vulnerabilities beyond their intrinsic characteristics. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

**Caution:** The `cvss4_threat_vector` object is currently only available for Vulnerability Management exports. It will be added to Web App Scanning findings exports soon.

threat_score.description: The CVSS v4.0 threat score (CVSS-T), which adjusts the base score by incorporating real-world threat intelligence, such as the presence of active exploitation, exploit code availability, or observed malware activity. This score reflects the current threat landscape for the vulnerability. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

exploit_maturity.description: The CVSS v4.0 Exploit Maturity (E) metric, indicating the current development status of exploit techniques or code for the vulnerability. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

raw.description: The complete `cvss4_threat_vector` metrics and their result values for the vulnerability, expressed as a concise, coded string. This threat vector is typically appended to the CVSSv4 Base vector. For example, `CVSS:4.0/E:U`. For more details, see the [CVSS v4.0 Specification](https://www.first.org/cvss/v4-0/specification-document).

PaginationResponse.description: An object containing pagination information for the current result set.

total.description: The total number of records matching your search criteria.

limit.description: The number of records requested (or the default value if omitted from the request).

offset.description: The starting record you requested (or zero if omitted).

sort.description: An array of objects specifying how the current result set is sorted.

PaginationResponseSortObject.description: An object specifying how the current result set is sorted, including the property by which the results were sorted and the sort direction.

name.description: The property used to sort the results.

order.description: The sort direction for the results. `asc` for ascending or `desc` for descending.

ErrorResponse.description: Tenable Web App Scanning error response.

reasons.description: A list of reasons for the Tenable Web App Scanning error.

items.description: A reason for the Tenable Web App Scanning error, including a code and an extended description.

code.description:
The Tenable Web App Scanning error code. Error code values include:
 - `NOT_FOUND`—Returned if Tenable Web App Scanning could not find the resource you specified.
 - `INVALID_ID_FORMAT`—Returned if you specify a resource ID in an invalid format.
 - `INVALID_PARAMETER`—Returned if you specify an invalid URL parameter.
- `INVALID_JSON_BODY`—Returned if you specify invalid JSON in request payload.
 - `DUPLICATE_ENTITY`—Returned if you attempt to create a duplicate resource.
 - `NOT_ALLOWED`—Returned if Tenable Web App Scanning encounters a stateful conflict, for example, if you attempt to start a scan that is already in progress.
 - `OPERATION_FORBIDDEN`—Returned if you do not have sufficient permissions to access a resource or complete a task.

reason.description: The extended description of the cause of the Tenable Web App Scanning error.

InputErrorResponse.description: Tenable Web App Scanning invalid input error response.

code.description: The Tenable Web App Scanning error code. The code `INPUT_FORM_VIOLATION` is returned if your request body contains invalid input.

fields.description: A list of fields that contain invalid input along with a message about the cause of the input error.

items.description: A reason for the Tenable Web App Scanning input error.

field.description: The JSON path of the invalid field.

message_type.description:
The type of input violation. Common input errors include:
 - `VALUE_MUST_BE_SPECIFIED`—Returned if a required body parameter is missing.
 - `VALUE_NOT_POSITIVE`—Returned if your input contains a negative integer when it should be positive.
 - `VALUE_IS_NEGATIVE`—Returned if your input contains a positive integer when it should be negative.
 - `VALUE_CANT_BE_CHANGED`—Returned if you attempted to change a value that cannot be changed.
 - `VALUE_MUST_BE_GREATER`—Returned if your input contains a value that is smaller than the minimum required lower bound.
 - `FIELD_CANT_BE_ADDED`—Returned if your input contains a field that can't be added.
 - `INVALID_TYPE`—Returned if your input contains an invalid type (ex. integer or string).
 - `INVALID_FORMAT`—Returned if your input contains an invalid format.

value.description: Contains an error message describing the cause of the input validation error.

user_defined_name.description: A unique, user-defined name for the web application asset.

url.description: The primary URL of the application. This can include the protocol, FQDN, port, and specific path to define the target's scope.

tags.description: A list of unique tag identifiers (UUIDs) to associate with the application. Tagging an application enables you to target it specifically when launching or scheduling scans.
